classes.name: Barbarian
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, Survival
classes.flavor: Primal forces of the land. Barbarians draw rage from the Weirding's chaos, ancient storms, natural fury, or the spirit of Hurstwold and Dollow Canyon.

subclasses.name: Path of the Howling Tempest
subclasses.description: Elemental winds and storms. You harness the power of hurricanes and tempests, becoming a living storm that tears through the battlefield.

features.name: Stormbound Rage
features.description: While raging: +10 ft. movement speed. If you move 10+ ft toward a creature and hit it with a melee attack, deal additional 1d6 thunder damage (2d6 at 10th, 3d6 at 14th). The 10-ft area around you becomes difficult terrain for enemies while raging.

features.name: Gale's Reprieve
features.description: Reaction: When a creature within 30 ft attacks you or an ally, push that target 10 ft in any direction. Does not provoke opportunity attacks. Uses: Constitution modifier (min 1) per long rest.

features.name: Tempest's Embrace
features.description: While raging: Resistance to lightning and thunder damage. When you take damage from a creature within 10 ft, that creature makes a Strength save (DC = 8 + proficiency + Constitution modifier) or is pushed 15 ft away.

features.name: Eye of the Storm
features.description: Action while raging: Summon a 20-ft-radius aura for 1 minute. While active: you and friendly creatures have advantage on Dexterity saving throws, ranged attacks against you have disadvantage, and all gain resistance to lightning and thunder damage. 1/long rest.

classes.name: Bard
classes.flavor: Storytellers, historians, diplomats, and conduits of ancient truths. Some channel the Weirding's echoes—'Weirding-Touched Bards' are seen as both geniuses and outcasts.

subclasses.name: College of Lanterns
subclasses.description: Harness the Weirding's strange energies through enchanted lanterns to reveal hidden truths, pierce illusions, and illuminate what truly is.

features.name: Guiding Light
features.description: Action: Summon a Weirdlight Lantern (floats nearby, bright light 30 ft + 30 ft dim, lasts 1 hour). Uses: Proficiency bonus per long rest. While active: Illusions falter in bright light (revealing true forms), Weirding-influenced creatures shimmer (advantage on Insight to understand them), and chosen creatures gain advantage on Perception checks and can't be surprised.

features.name: Lantern of Clarity
features.description: While Weirdlight Lantern is active: Advantage on saves against fear, charm, and confusion. Bonus action: Reveal one hidden magical or Weirding influence within 30 ft (visible until end of next turn). Also: Cast Detect Magic or Identify once per long rest without a spell slot while lantern is active.

features.name: Radiant Lantern of the Weirding
features.description: When summoning your lantern, optionally activate one effect (1/long rest): Weirdlight Surge (Wisdom save or dazed), Lantern of Duality (temporarily revert Weirded objects/creatures to pre-Weirded state), or Weirding Resonance (extra psychic damage = Charisma modifier to spell/magic damage targets inside light).

classes.name: Cleric
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: History, Insight, Medicine, Persuasion, Religion
classes.flavor: Divine magic behaves unpredictably in Weird Zones. Clerics serve as stabilizers, spiritual guides, or explorers of the Weirding's distortions.

subclasses.name: Domain of Resonance
subclasses.description: Attuned to the magical frequencies between the material and astral planes. You maintain balance between order and chaos through sound, vibration, and perception.

features.name: Vibrational Insight
features.description: Gain proficiency in Performance OR Arcana. Cast Detect Magic at will as a ritual. When used this way, you hear vibrations that reveal the aura and intent of magical items or beings.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Amplify Vibration
features.description: Action: Create a resonant pulse in a 30-ft radius for 1 minute. All spell damage in area gains +1d6 thunder damage. The next enemy to fail a save must make a Constitution save or become dazed (incapacitated until end of their next turn). Can end early as a bonus action.

features.name: Harmonic Surge
features.description: Reaction when you or an ally within 30 ft casts a spell: grant one of—Harmonic Boost (+1d8 thunder damage to spell's effect), or Resonant Echo (if target moves next turn, it takes 1d8 psychic damage). Uses: Wisdom modifier (min 1) per long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike (Thunder)
features.description: Once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, deal +1d8 thunder damage. Increases to +2d8 at 14th level.

features.name: Cosmic Resonance
features.description: Action: Create a 60-ft sphere for 1 minute (1/long rest). Allies' spell attacks and save DCs +2. Enemies have disadvantage on saves vs. thunder or psychic damage. Planar Feedback: If an enemy fails a save inside the field, they become vulnerable to thunder damage until end of your next turn.

classes.name: Druid
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Arcana, Animal Handling, Insight, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Religion, Survival
classes.flavor: More than protectors of nature—intermediaries between natural and unnatural. The Weirding has unsettled druids, splitting them into Ancient Guardians, Wayfarers, and Weird-Touched Mystics.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Weird
subclasses.description: Embrace the chaotic, unstable energies between the material and astral planes. Use the Weirding's influence to reshape the battlefield, adapt to bizarre forms, and reveal hidden truths.

features.name: Weird Shape
features.description: Bonus action: Expend a Wild Shape use to assume a Weirded Beast form. At the start of each turn, roll d6 OR keep previous result: 1=+10 ft reach, 2=+2 AC (phase), 3=Illusions (attackers disadvantage on Wisdom save), 4=1d6 fire/cold to attackers, 5=Advantage on Perception, 6=Voice twists (Intimidation advantage + 1d6 psychic to listeners).

features.name: Revelation of the Real
features.description: Cast Light at will. When cast this way, the light reveals the true nature of Weirding-affected creatures and objects. When concentrating on a light spell (Daylight, Faerie Fire), illuminated creatures have disadvantage on saves against your druid spells.

features.name: Twist the Unseen
features.description: When using Wild Shape, choose one effect: Flicker Between (resistance to all damage, move through creatures/objects as difficult terrain for 1 minute), or Illusory Echo (create duplicate; creatures make DC 15 Intelligence save to identify you).

features.name: Weird Manifestation
features.description: Action: Unleash a 30-ft radius Weird Manifestation (1/long rest). Choose 2 effects or roll d8: 1=No reactions for 1 min, 2=Gravity shifts (push/pull 10 ft), 3=Frightened on Wisdom save, 4=Disadvantage on attacks for 1 min, 5=Difficult terrain spreads, 6=Objects levitate, 7=All creatures regain 2d8 HP, 8=Invisible creatures forced visible for 1 min.

classes.name: Fighter
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Athletics, History, Insight, Intimidation, Perception, Survival
classes.flavor: Disciplined warriors, gladiators, knights, airship crew, and Kigan Uldar champions. The Weirding has forced adaptation of combat techniques.

subclasses.name: Skybound Captain
subclasses.description: Tactical master of airship command and aerial combat. Your leadership, aerial maneuvering, and lightning-fast decisions inspire crews and terrify foes.

features.name: Airship Command
features.description: Gain proficiency with Navigator's tools and vehicles (air). Sky Orders (bonus action): Give a tactical order to an ally within 30 ft—they gain advantage on their next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Uses: Proficiency bonus per long rest. Acrobatic Proficiency: Gain Acrobatics proficiency (or expertise if already proficient).

features.name: Windborne Maneuvering
features.description: When you take the Dash action, you can make one weapon attack as part of that action. Ignore non-magical difficult terrain. Move through the space of friendly creatures without extra movement cost.

features.name: Captain's Aura
features.description: 10-ft radius leadership aura (expands to 30 ft at 18th level): Allies add your proficiency bonus to initiative rolls. Allies operating air/water vehicles add your proficiency bonus to navigation and control skill checks.

features.name: Eye of the Storm
features.description: On initiative roll, you can swap your result with a willing ally within 30 ft. If an ally within 10 ft must make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to grant them advantage.

features.name: Stormlord's Wrath
features.description: 1/long rest: Summon an electrical storm for 1 minute. Enemies have disadvantage on ranged attacks against your crew. Airship speed +10 ft. You and allies within 30 ft deal extra 2d8 lightning/thunder damage on one attack per turn. While aboard an airship, you and allies gain resistance to lightning and thunder damage.

classes.name: Monk
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Acrobatics, Athletics, History, Insight, Religion, Stealth
classes.flavor: Channel the physical and metaphysical into harmony. Elemental balance and ancient philosophy guide their practice. Some orders are infused by the Weirding.

subclasses.name: Way of Shifting Powers
subclasses.description: Channel and manipulate volatile elemental resonance. You are a vessel for elemental energy that ebbs and flows through you with each strike.

features.name: Resonant Strike
features.description: When making an unarmed strike, infuse it with shifting resonance. Choose an energy type (fire, cold, thunder, or radiant). The attack deals additional 1d6 damage of the chosen type. The energy type can change with each unarmed strike.

features.name: Flux Step
features.description: Bonus action (1 Ki point): Teleport up to 20 ft to a visible location. When you reappear, creatures within 5 ft make a Dexterity save (DC = 8 + proficiency + Wisdom modifier) or take 2d6 force damage.

features.name: Elemental Confluence
features.description: At the start of each turn, attune to an element: Fire (+1d8 fire damage on attacks), Air (+10 ft movement + Acrobatics advantage), Water (heal self or ally within 10 ft for 1d8 as bonus action), or Earth (gain temporary HP equal to Wisdom modifier). Change attunement at start of each turn freely.

features.name: Avatar of Resonance
features.description: Action (5 Ki points): Enter perfect elemental harmony for 1 minute (1/long rest). Resistance to all non-psychic damage. Each Attack action lets you switch resonance mid-attack for different elemental bonuses on each hit. Teleport up to 30 ft as bonus action, leaving a 3d8 force blast at departure point.

classes.name: Paladin
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Athletics, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Persuasion, Religion
classes.flavor: Champions of conviction. The Weirding can corrupt broken oaths, twisting powers in unforeseen ways. Some patrol Weirded zones as reality anchors.

subclasses.name: Oath of Resonance
subclasses.description: Maintain harmony between the forces of Ryndor. You are a guardian of equilibrium—neither imposing rigid order nor chaos, but preserving the balance.
tenets: Flow Like Water, Stand Like Stone

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Resonant Strike (bonus action): Infuse weapon with elemental energy—next hit deals +2d10 damage (choose fire/cold/lightning/radiant) and pushes target 10 ft. OR Field of Alignment (action): Create aura within 10 ft for 1 minute; creatures inside can reroll saves against conditions.

features.name: Aura of Equilibrium
features.description: 10-ft radius (30 ft at 18th level): When a creature rolls with advantage or disadvantage, treat both rolls as equal to the higher of the two values.

features.name: Resonant Reflection
features.description: Reaction to elemental effect (fire/cold/lightning/etc.): Take half damage; your next melee attack within 1 minute deals the absorbed damage type as extra damage.

features.name: Avatar of Balance
features.description: Action (1/long rest): Become a beacon of cosmic harmony for 1 minute. Resistance to all damage. Creatures within 30 ft cannot have advantage or disadvantage. Change damage type of any attack to fire, cold, radiant, or necrotic.

subclasses.name: Oath of Discord
subclasses.description: Revel in chaos, destruction, and entropy. These Paladins reject balance, serving malevolent forces or entropy itself. They are agents of glorious ruin.
tenets: Embrace Power (seize it by any means)

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Agony's Touch (bonus action): Curse a creature within 30 ft—for 1 minute, they take +1d8 necrotic damage whenever they take damage. OR Seed of Discord (action): Two creatures within 30 ft must succeed on a Wisdom save or be compelled to attack one another for 1 minute.

features.name: Aura of Strife
features.description: 10-ft radius (30 ft at 18th level): Creatures within cannot benefit from temporary HP. When a creature in the aura makes a melee attack, it deals extra damage equal to your Charisma modifier (min +1).

features.name: Harbinger of Betrayal
features.description: Resistance to psychic damage. Reaction when an ally within 10 ft makes a saving throw: That ally automatically fails; you gain 2d8 temporary HP.

features.name: Avatar of Chaos
features.description: Action (1/long rest): Enter full power of discord for 1 minute. Creatures within 30 ft have disadvantage on saving throws. When a creature takes damage in your aura, another creature of your choice within range also takes half that damage. Once per turn on a hit, the target becomes frightened until end of your next turn.

classes.name: Ranger
classes.skills: Choose 3 from: Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Investigation, Nature, Perception, Stealth, Survival
classes.flavor: Masters of survival and navigation. They blend ancient traditions with elemental resonance and primal intuition. Archetypes include Skyward Stalkers, Echo Wardens, and Lantern Guides.

subclasses.name: Arcane Mechanist
subclasses.description: Merge nature's mastery with technological ingenuity. You use and maintain advanced tools, airships, and mechanical companions.

features.name: Tinker's Tools & Tech Sense
features.description: Gain proficiency with Tinker's Tools. Action: Detect magical or technological traps, contraptions, and energy sources within 30 ft. You have advantage on checks to dismantle, repair, or understand mechanical devices.

features.name: Mechanical Companion
features.description: Construct a small mechanical companion (similar to a ranger's beast companion). It follows your commands, can scout, and has limited combat capability. Repair on a short rest using Tinker's Tools.

features.name: Arcane Gadgets
features.description: Craft gadgets using Tinker's Tools. You can maintain a number of gadgets equal to your proficiency bonus. Examples: Flashbang (blind in 10-ft radius), Grappling Hook Launcher (reach up to 60 ft), Arcane Shield (absorb 2d6 damage once), Smoke Bomb (obscure 10-ft area).

features.name: Arcane Infusion
features.description: Infuse one weapon or piece of ammunition with arcane energy. Grants +1 to attack and damage rolls and adds 1d6 force damage. Lasts until you use this feature on a new item.

features.name: Master Mechanist
features.description: Your mechanical companion gains the ability to act independently (makes attacks and uses abilities on its own turn). Gadgets become more powerful (double the effect or area). Once per long rest, activate Overclock: double the mechanical companion's speed and damage for 1 minute.

classes.name: Rogue
classes.skills: Choose 4 from: Acrobatics, Athletics, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Perception, Performance, Persuasion, Sleight of Hand, Stealth
classes.flavor: Thieves, assassins, spies, and opportunists. In Ryndor, rogues navigate the complex politics of cities like Eveltoft and Surreymouth.

subclasses.name: Riftstalker
subclasses.description: Harness dimensional energy to slip between spaces in reality. You strike from impossible angles, teleporting through the battlefield and attacking through the fabric of space itself.

features.name: Rift Step
features.description: Bonus action: Teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. If you teleport adjacent to a creature, your next attack before end of turn gains advantage and deals +2d6 force damage. Uses: Proficiency bonus per long rest.

features.name: Phase Strike
features.description: When you use Rift Step, you can make a Sneak Attack through the rift even if you don't have advantage, as long as you don't have disadvantage. Additionally, Sneak Attack can be dealt as force damage.

features.name: Spatial Anchor
features.description: Reaction when a creature within 60 ft tries to teleport or plane shift: Make an Intelligence save contested by their spell/ability. On success, their teleportation fails. Uses: Wisdom modifier (min 1) per long rest.

features.name: Rift Mastery
features.description: Teleportation distance increases to 60 ft. Once per turn, when you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can teleport the target up to 10 ft in any direction. Rift Step no longer has a usage limit.

classes.name: Sorcerer
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Arcana, Deception, Insight, Intimidation, Persuasion, Religion
classes.flavor: Magic flows through them unbidden and untamed. In Ryndor, the Rift's influence can twist sorcerous power in strange and terrible ways.

subclasses.name: Riftborn Origin
subclasses.description: Your magic is born from exposure to—or origin within—the Rift between planes. Your power is volatile, unpredictable, and carries echoes of Nebernoria itself.

features.name: Rift Surge
features.description: When you cast a spell using a spell slot, roll d20. On a natural 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table OR the Rift Surge table (d6): 1=Reality tears (target teleported 10 ft in random direction), 2=Dimensional echo (duplicate caster in adjacent space for 1 round), 3=Temporal blur (next attack against you has disadvantage), 4=Spatial inversion (gravity inverts 10-ft area for 1 round), 5=Rift whispers (target hears voices, disadvantage on next concentration save), 6=Power surge (next spell deals +2d6 force damage).

features.name: Dimensional Instincts
features.description: You have a natural sense for spatial anomalies. You can't be surprised if you're not incapacitated. You have advantage on saves against being teleported against your will or banished.

features.name: Rift Empowerment
features.description: Spend 2 Sorcery Points: Next spell ignores resistance to its damage type. Spend 3 Sorcery Points: Add your Charisma modifier as force damage to a spell's damage roll.

features.name: Spatial Mastery
features.description: Misty Step costs no spell slot (1/short rest). Dimension Door costs no spell slot (1/long rest). You can take other creatures with you through Dimension Door (up to Charisma modifier, min 1).

features.name: Tear Reality
features.description: Action (1/long rest): Tear a rift in reality in a 30-ft radius for 1 minute. Creatures of your choice take 8d8 force damage at start of their turns (Constitution save for half). The area is difficult terrain. You can teleport anywhere within the area as a bonus action.

classes.name: Warlock
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Arcana, Deception, History, Intimidation, Investigation, Nature, Religion
classes.flavor: Power comes with a price—always. In Ryndor, Warlocks often make pacts with entities from beyond the Rift, beings of impossible hunger and alien design.

subclasses.name: Pact of the Planebinder
subclasses.description: You have made a pact with one of five Rift Patrons—entities of immense power from beyond the Rift. Your patron shapes your powers and what it demands in return.

patrons.name: The Blighted Conductor
patrons.description: A being of corrupted music and infectious entropy. Grants powers over disease, sound, and cascading decay.

patrons.name: The Endless Dreamer
patrons.description: An alien consciousness lost in perpetual dream. Grants powers over illusion, sleep, and the boundary between waking and dreaming.

patrons.name: The Shattered One
patrons.description: A fractured god-thing broken across multiple planes. Grants powers over division, fracture, and impossible geometry.

patrons.name: The Hunger Between Stars
patrons.description: An ancient void-entity of pure consumption. Grants powers over necrotic energy, fear, and absolute emptiness.

patrons.name: The Many-Mouthed Mother
patrons.description: A vast plural entity of insatiable appetite and reproduction. Grants powers over swarms, transformation, and consuming growth.

features.name: Patron's Mark
features.description: Your patron brands you with a mark of their essence. Choose your Rift Patron. You gain a unique cantrip tied to their nature and resistance to one damage type appropriate to your patron.

features.name: Rift Communion
features.description: Once per long rest, enter a trance to commune with your patron. Ask up to 3 questions (yes/no, or cryptic hints for complex questions). The patron may also impose a compulsion or demand in exchange.

features.name: Planebinder's Power
features.description: Your patron grants you expanded power. You learn one spell from any spell list that reflects your patron's nature (DM approval). This spell counts as a Warlock spell for you and doesn't count against your spells known.

features.name: Avatar of the Rift
features.description: 1/long rest: Channel your patron's essence for 1 minute. You gain resistance to all damage, your Eldritch Blast deals +2d10 damage of your patron's damage type, and creatures that start their turn within 10 ft take psychic damage equal to your Charisma modifier.

classes.name: Wizard
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Arcana, History, Insight, Investigation, Medicine, Religion
classes.flavor: Students of arcane truth, Crystal Scholars, and masters of magical theory. In Ryndor, the Weirding has created new fields of arcane research.

subclasses.name: School of Harmonics
subclasses.description: Study the resonant frequencies underlying all magic. Harmonics wizards understand that all spells are vibrations—and by tuning those vibrations, they can amplify, redirect, or disrupt them.

features.name: Resonant Pulse
features.description: When you deal damage with a spell, you can spend 1 spell slot (of any level) as a bonus action to send a resonant pulse through the target. The target takes 1d8 thunder damage per level of the slot spent, and must make a Constitution save or lose concentration.

features.name: Tune the Arcane
features.description: When you copy a spell into your spellbook, the cost is halved. When you cast a spell, you can change its damage type to thunder, lightning, or psychic without expending additional resources.

features.name: Harmonic Ward
features.description: As a reaction when you take damage, create a harmonic barrier absorbing 2d8 + Intelligence modifier damage. The absorbed energy amplifies your next spell: add the absorbed damage amount as bonus thunder damage on your next damaging spell. Uses: Proficiency bonus per long rest.

features.name: Resonant Attunement
features.description: You gain proficiency in Performance skill (or expertise if already proficient). You can concentrate on two spells at once, but must make a DC 15 Constitution save at the start of each turn or lose concentration on one of your choice.

features.name: Symphony of Elements
features.description: Action (1/long rest): Conduct a symphony of elemental forces for 1 minute. Each round, choose one effect: Crescendo (all spells deal +2d8 of any elemental damage), Harmony (allies in 30 ft gain +2 to spell attack rolls), Dissonance (enemies in 30 ft have disadvantage on concentration saves), or Finale (end the symphony with a 30-ft burst of 8d8 thunder damage, Constitution save for half).

classes.name: Sev'rinn
classes.skills: Choose 2 from: Arcana, Athletics, Nature, Perception, Survival
classes.flavor: Living conduits of elemental power. Sev'rinn command elemental magic with instinct rather than study, adapting moment by moment. Unlike Wizards or Sorcerers, they align with elemental forces directly. Every spark of power risks triggering a Weirding Surge.
classes.special_note: This is a brand-new class unique to Ryndor. The sourcebook notes this class is 'Under Construction' — some techniques and multiclassing rules are still being developed. Collaborate with your GM.

mechanics.elemental_charges: You regain all Elemental Charges on a short or long rest. Charges power your Combat Techniques and Elemental Shift.
mechanics.weirding_surge: Whenever you spend Charges, roll a d6. The result on the Weirding Surge Table occurs immediately. Multiple Charges spent in one action still require only one roll.
weirding_surge_table: A rift briefly opens, summoning a random creature for 1 minute.
Gravity reverses in a 10-ft radius around you for 1 round.
A harmless glowing orb follows you for 10 minutes.
Terrain around you shifts—difficult terrain for 1 round.
Your voice echoes unnaturally—disadvantage on Charisma checks.
Time flickers—your next action repeats itself automatically.

elemental_shift.activation: Bonus action, spend 1 Charge. Lasts 1 minute or until you end it as a free action on your turn.
elemental_shift.lock_mode: Choose one effect from your attunement's Shift Table. It lasts for the full duration.
elemental_shift.roll_mode: Roll 1d6 and apply the result. At the start of each of your turns, you may keep the current effect or re-roll freely (no Charge cost).
elemental_shift.shift_discount: While your Shift is active, all Combat Techniques cost 1 fewer Charge (minimum 1).
elemental_shift.reentry: End freely on your turn; re-activate anytime for 1 Charge. No limit per encounter.

class_features.name: Resonant Flow
class_features.description: Once per turn, if you roll a natural 18, 19, or 20 on an attack roll or ability check after spending a Charge, you immediately regain 1 Charge. This cannot push you above your maximum.

class_features.name: Elemental Avatar
class_features.description: While this feature is active, you can change which attunement's Shift Table you are using at the start of each turn for free. This does not waive the 1-Charge cost to activate the Shift itself.

subclasses.name: Tideborn (Water Attunement)
subclasses.description: Command the relentless power of water—healing, restraining, and sweeping enemies away like the tide.

shift_table.name: Tidal Surge
shift_table.effect: +15 ft movement; move through enemy spaces without OA; spaces you exit become difficult terrain for enemies.

shift_table.name: Riptide Grasp
shift_table.effect: On melee hit, pull target 10 ft. If they fail Strength save, knocked prone.

shift_table.name: Mistform Cloak
shift_table.effect: Resistance to non-magical weapon damage; ranged attacks from 10+ ft with disadvantage.

shift_table.name: Icy Flow
shift_table.effect: 10-ft radius ice sheet under your feet; difficult terrain for enemies; enemies moving through save or fall prone.

shift_table.name: Healing Current
shift_table.effect: At start of each turn, ally within 15 ft regains 1d8 + Wisdom mod HP.

shift_table.name: Crashing Blow
shift_table.effect: Melee attacks deal extra 1d10 bludgeoning; push target 10 ft back.

techniques.name: Tidal Nudge
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you send a pulse of water at a creature within 30 feet. It must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier) or be pushed 5 feet in any direction you choose.

techniques.name: Misting Veil
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you shroud yourself in a fine spray of water vapor. Until the start of your next turn, ranged attacks made against you are made with disadvantage.

techniques.name: Frostbite Touch
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you reach out and channel biting cold into a creature within 5 feet. The target takes 1d6 cold damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Aqua Pulse
techniques.description: As a reaction when you take damage from a melee attack, you release a burst of water. The attacker takes 1d4 bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet back.

techniques.name: Slick Step
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you coat the ground in a 10-foot-radius area within 15 feet of you with a thin sheet of water. The area becomes difficult terrain until the start of your next turn. Creatures that move through it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone.

techniques.name: Chill Grasp
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can channel icy water into the strike, dealing an extra 1d8 cold damage. The target's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of its next turn.

techniques.name: Undertow Strike
techniques.description: As a bonus action immediately after hitting a creature with a melee attack, you drag a current of water beneath them. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

techniques.name: Tide Wall
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you summon a 5-foot-wide, 10-foot-tall wall of surging water at a point within 15 feet of you. The wall provides half cover to anyone behind it and lasts until the start of your next turn. Creatures that attempt to move through it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have their movement stopped.

techniques.name: Mending Current
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you channel a restorative current of water into a creature within 15 feet, restoring 1d6 + Wisdom modifier hit points.

techniques.name: Maelstrom Hold
techniques.description: When you initiate or maintain a grapple on a creature, churning water batters it. The grappled target takes 1d8 bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns.

techniques.name: Crashing Wave
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you launch a crashing wave at a target within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 3d6 bludgeoning damage, be pushed 10 feet away, and be knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage and are not moved.

techniques.name: Icy Embrace
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you summon a burst of frigid water around a target within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be restrained by ice for 1 minute. They may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns.

techniques.name: Saltwater Shield
techniques.description: As a reaction when you are hit by an attack, you surround yourself in a dense barrier of water that absorbs 2d6 + Constitution modifier damage from the triggering hit.

techniques.name: Whirlpool Step
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you dissolve into a swirl of water and reappear in an unoccupied space you can see within 20 feet. Creatures within 5 feet of the space you left must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled 5 feet toward that space.

techniques.name: Numbing Tide
techniques.description: As a bonus action before you make a weapon attack, you coat your weapon in icy water. On a hit, the attack deals cold damage instead of its normal type, and the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

techniques.name: Riptide Step
techniques.description: You teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, leaving behind a whirlpool of water. Creatures within 5 feet of your starting point must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled 10 feet toward that space.

techniques.name: Healing Waters
techniques.description: You create a restorative pool in a 10-foot radius centered on a point within 30 feet. Allies in the pool regain hit points equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier at the start of their turns for up to 1 minute.

techniques.name: Blizzard Barrage
techniques.description: As an action, you launch three shards of ice at up to three creatures within 60 feet (one per creature). Make a ranged spell attack for each (using Wisdom modifier + proficiency bonus). Each shard deals 2d6 cold damage on a hit.

techniques.name: Stormtide Surge
techniques.description: As a bonus action, a wave of water rushes out in a 15-foot cone from you. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 2d6 bludgeoning damage and be pushed 10 feet back.

techniques.name: Aqua Form
techniques.description: As a bonus action, your body becomes partially liquid for 1 minute. You can move through spaces as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing, you are immune to the grappled and restrained conditions from nonmagical sources, and melee attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

techniques.name: Torrent Shield
techniques.description: As a reaction to being hit by an attack, you summon a barrier of water around yourself or an ally within 10 feet. The barrier absorbs damage equal to 1d12 + your Wisdom modifier before dissipating.

techniques.name: Floodgate Surge
techniques.description: As an action, you unleash a massive wave in a 30-foot cone. All creatures in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone and take 3d8 bludgeoning damage. On a success, they take half damage and are not knocked prone.

techniques.name: Tidal Imprisonment
techniques.description: As an action, you surround a creature within 30 feet in a sphere of churning water 10 feet in diameter. The creature is restrained and begins suffocating. At the end of each of its turns, it may make a Strength saving throw to break free. The sphere lasts 1 minute or until the creature escapes.

techniques.name: Glacial Eruption
techniques.description: As an action, you slam the ground and cause a burst of ice to erupt in a 15-foot radius centered on a point within 60 feet. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d8 cold damage and be restrained by ice until the end of their next turn. On a success, they take half damage and are not restrained.

techniques.name: Tidewalker
techniques.description: For 10 minutes, you gain a swim speed equal to your walking speed + 30 feet and can breathe underwater. Allies within 10 feet of you also gain a swim speed equal to their walking speed.

techniques.name: Tide of Renewal
techniques.description: You create a healing surge that spreads in a 20-foot radius. All allies within the radius regain 2d10 hit points, and one condition of their choice (such as poisoned or frightened) is removed.

techniques.name: Whirlpool Prison
techniques.description: You summon a massive whirlpool centered on a point within 60 feet — a 10-foot radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder. Any creature that starts its turn inside must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained and pulled to the center. A restrained creature may attempt the saving throw at the end of its turn. The whirlpool lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it.

techniques.name: Deep Current
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you call a powerful underwater current beneath the battlefield. For 1 minute, all creatures on the ground must succeed on a Strength saving throw at the start of their turn or be moved 20 feet in a direction of your choice.

techniques.name: Permafrost
techniques.description: You touch the ground, and ice spreads in a 30-foot radius from you. For 1 minute, the area is frozen: difficult terrain for all creatures except you and your allies. Any creature that starts its turn in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone.

techniques.name: Drowning Pull
techniques.description: As an action, you project a column of rushing water at a creature within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be dragged 30 feet toward you, take 4d10 bludgeoning damage, and become incapacitated until the end of its next turn. On a success, it takes half damage and is not moved.

techniques.name: Ocean's Dominion
techniques.description: You summon the power of an oceanic storm, creating heavy rain and strong winds in a 60-foot radius for 1 minute. While active, creatures of your choice have advantage on Dexterity and Strength saving throws. Enemies treat the area as difficult terrain, and ranged attacks within the storm are made with disadvantage.

techniques.name: Tsunami Strike
techniques.description: You unleash a massive wave that sweeps across a 60-foot line. Creatures caught in the wave must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 8d10 bludgeoning damage and be swept 20 feet away. On a success, they take half damage and remain standing.

techniques.name: Abyssal Vortex
techniques.description: As an action, you tear open a vortex of water in a 20-foot radius centered on a point within 120 feet. For 1 minute, all creatures in the radius take 4d10 bludgeoning damage at the start of their turns (Strength saving throw halves) and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled 10 feet toward the center.

techniques.name: Flash Freeze
techniques.description: As an action, you flash-freeze everything in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 10d8 cold damage and be frozen solid (paralyzed) for 1 minute (they may repeat the save at the end of each of their turns). On a success, they take half damage and are not paralyzed.

techniques.name: Living Sea
techniques.description: As an action, you merge with water for 1 hour, becoming a semi-liquid being. You are immune to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage; can pass through any space large enough for water to flow; and gain a swim speed of 120 feet. Once per turn as a bonus action, you can erupt in a burst of force, dealing 4d8 bludgeoning damage to all creatures within 10 feet of you (Strength saving throw halves).

features.name: Tideform
features.description: When you activate your Elemental Shift, you partially meld with the essence of water. While your Shift is active: you gain a swim speed equal to your walking speed and can breathe underwater; ranged attacks made against you from more than 10 feet away are made with disadvantage, as currents deflect incoming projectiles; once per turn, you can move through a space occupied by another creature without provoking opportunity attacks.

subclasses.name: Skycaller (Air Attunement)
subclasses.description: Ride the winds of change, striking from the sky with blinding speed and disorienting force.

shift_table.name: Cyclone's Touch
shift_table.effect: +10 ft movement; opportunity attacks against you with disadvantage.

shift_table.name: Whisper of Winds
shift_table.effect: Cast Silent Image or Message at will without spell slot.

shift_table.name: Shockwave Burst
shift_table.effect: Reaction when taking damage: push enemies within 10 ft back 5 ft.

shift_table.name: Gale Guard
shift_table.effect: +2 AC.

shift_table.name: Zephyr Step
shift_table.effect: Move through enemy spaces without OA; movement does not provoke reactions.

shift_table.name: Tempest Strike
shift_table.effect: On melee hit, knock target prone OR push them 10 ft back.

techniques.name: Gust Push
techniques.description: As a bonus action, a targeted creature within 20 feet must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet in a direction of your choice.

techniques.name: Wind Buffer
techniques.description: As a reaction when you are targeted by a ranged attack, you deflect the attack with a sudden gust. The attack is made with disadvantage.

techniques.name: Tailwind
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you surround yourself with rushing air. Your movement speed increases by 10 feet until the end of your turn, and you can move through difficult terrain without penalty this turn.

techniques.name: Sonic Clap
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you slam your hands together with explosive force. Each creature within 5 feet must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 thunder damage and be deafened until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Aerobatics
techniques.description: As a reaction to falling, you catch yourself on the air. You slow your descent to 60 feet per round and take no damage when you land, no matter the height. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

techniques.name: Wind Dance
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you can Dash, and until the end of your turn, you ignore difficult terrain and opportunity attacks.

techniques.name: Lightning Lash
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you snap a whip of lightning at a creature within 15 feet. Make a ranged spell attack (Wisdom modifier + proficiency bonus). On a hit, the target takes 2d6 lightning damage and cannot take reactions until the start of its next turn.

techniques.name: Cyclone Guard
techniques.description: As a reaction to being hit by a melee attack, you conjure a blast of wind. Reduce the damage of the triggering hit by 1d8 + Wisdom modifier and push the attacker 5 feet back.

techniques.name: Updraft
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you or a willing creature within 5 feet is launched up to 20 feet into the air on a burst of wind. Until the end of that creature's next turn, it ignores opportunity attacks and can move through the air as though on solid ground.

techniques.name: Wailing Wind
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you emit a shrieking gust. All creatures within 10 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of their next turn.

techniques.name: Air Slash
techniques.description: When you make a weapon attack, you can extend your strike with a sharp gust of wind. Make a ranged weapon attack (30 ft.). On a hit, it deals your weapon's damage type plus 1d8 slashing damage.

techniques.name: Thunderbolt Strike
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you charge your next weapon attack with crackling electricity. On a hit, the attack deals an extra 2d8 lightning damage, and the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

techniques.name: Pressure Wave
techniques.description: As an action, you release a concentrated burst of compressed air in a 30-foot line, 5 feet wide. Creatures in the area take 3d6 thunder damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

techniques.name: Ride the Storm
techniques.description: As a bonus action, for 1 minute your movement no longer provokes opportunity attacks, and you can move through the spaces of hostile creatures without penalty.

techniques.name: Lightning Step
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you move up to 30 feet in a straight line in a streak of electricity. Each creature whose space you move through takes 1d6 lightning damage (Dexterity saving throw halves).

techniques.name: Storm's Wake
techniques.description: When you successfully land an attack, your target is surrounded by a gust of wind. Until the start of your next turn, the target's movement is halved and it has disadvantage on ranged attack rolls.

techniques.name: Thunderhead
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you summon a crackling stormcloud in a 20-foot radius, 30 feet above a point within 60 feet. For 1 minute, at the start of each of your turns, lightning strikes a creature of your choice beneath it, dealing 2d8 lightning damage (Dexterity saving throw halves).

techniques.name: Gale Eruption
techniques.description: As an action, you emit a massive burst of wind in a 20-foot radius centered on you. All creatures in the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from you and knocked prone.

techniques.name: Zephyr Cloak
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you wrap yourself in a constant gale for 1 minute. All ranged weapon attacks against you are made with disadvantage, and you gain a +2 bonus to AC against melee attacks.

techniques.name: Wind Tunnel
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you create a 60-foot-long, 10-foot-wide tunnel of howling wind in a direction you choose. Creatures in the tunnel must succeed on Strength saving throws or be pushed 20 feet in the direction the wind blows. Allies you designate are unaffected. The tunnel lasts until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Flight Mastery
techniques.description: You learn to ride the winds effortlessly. As a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 30 feet for 1 minute.

techniques.name: Eye of the Storm
techniques.description: As an action, you become the calm center of a raging vortex for 1 minute. You are immune to all forced movement. All creatures within 20 feet of you treat the area as difficult terrain and have disadvantage on ranged attacks while within the storm.

techniques.name: Chain Lightning
techniques.description: As an action, you release a bolt of lightning at a creature within 60 feet (ranged spell attack). On a hit, it arcs to up to three additional creatures within 30 feet of the first target, dealing 3d8 lightning damage to the original target and 2d8 to each subsequent one.

techniques.name: Skyward Slam
techniques.description: As a bonus action immediately after hitting a creature with a melee attack, you channel a vertical burst of air beneath the target. It is launched 20 feet upward. If it hits a ceiling or falls, it takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage from the impact and lands prone.

techniques.name: Resonant Thunder
techniques.description: As an action, you unleash a deafening boom in a 30-foot radius. All creatures in the area take 4d8 thunder damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be deafened for 1 minute.

techniques.name: Howling Strike
techniques.description: When you make a melee weapon attack, you can choose to summon a thunderous boom. The attack deals an extra 2d6 thunder damage, and creatures within 5 feet of the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away.

techniques.name: Storm Avatar
techniques.description: As an action, for 1 minute your body crackles with uncontained lightning. You gain immunity to lightning and thunder damage. Any creature that hits you with a melee attack takes 2d6 lightning damage. Your weapon attacks deal an extra 1d8 lightning damage.

techniques.name: Hurricane Force
techniques.description: As an action, you create a field of hurricane-force winds in a 40-foot radius centered on you for 1 minute. Ranged attacks within the area automatically miss. Large or smaller creatures must succeed on Strength saving throws at the start of their turns or be knocked prone. All movement through the area costs double.

techniques.name: Static Field
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you charge the air around you for 1 minute. Whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack, it takes 2d8 lightning damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or lose its reaction until the start of its next turn.

techniques.name: Whirlwind Ascent
techniques.description: As an action, you spin into a roaring cyclone for 1 minute. You gain a fly speed of 60 feet. When you move through a creature's space, that creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d8 slashing damage (half on success).

techniques.name: Supercell
techniques.description: As an action, you call down a catastrophic storm in a 120-foot radius centered on you for 1 minute. At the start of each of your turns, all enemies within the radius take 2d10 lightning damage and 2d10 thunder damage (Dexterity saving throw halves). Allies are unaffected.

techniques.name: Tornado
techniques.description: As an action, you summon a massive tornado in a 30-foot-radius, 100-foot-tall column centered on a point within 60 feet. For 1 minute, all creatures entering or starting their turn in the area take 6d10 bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be hurled 40 feet in a random direction.

techniques.name: Skyshatter
techniques.description: As an action, you call down five bolts of lightning within a 60-foot radius centered on a point you can see. You assign each bolt to a target of your choice within the area. Each bolt deals 4d12 lightning damage (Dexterity saving throw halves). On a failed save, the target is also stunned until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Galeform
techniques.description: As an action, for 1 hour you become a being of pure wind. You gain immunity to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage; a fly speed of 90 feet; and the ability to pass through any space large enough for air. Melee attacks against you automatically miss. Once per turn as a bonus action, deal 4d8 bludgeoning damage to all enemies within 20 feet (Dexterity saving throw halves).

techniques.name: Thunderous Reckoning
techniques.description: As an action, you unleash every storm within your soul. All creatures within 60 feet of you take 8d10 thunder damage and 4d12 lightning damage (Dexterity saving throw halves). On a failed save, they are also deafened, blinded, and knocked prone for 1 minute (repeated saves at the end of each turn).

features.name: Windform
features.description: When you activate your Elemental Shift, winds gather around you and you become something between flesh and air. While your Shift is active: you gain a flying speed of 10 feet (hover); you cannot be knocked prone — effects that would knock you prone fail; you have resistance to lightning and thunder damage.

subclasses.name: Lithomage (Stone Attunement)
subclasses.description: Become an immovable fortress of elemental stone, protecting allies and crushing all who stand against you.

shift_table.name: Granite Skin
shift_table.effect: Temporary HP = level + Constitution modifier.

shift_table.name: Earthen Anchor
shift_table.effect: Movement cannot be reduced; cannot be knocked prone.

shift_table.name: Stonegrasp
shift_table.effect: Bonus action: ground grasps a creature (Strength save or restrained until end of next turn).

shift_table.name: Seismic Aura
shift_table.effect: Enemy starting turn within 5 ft makes Dex save or pushed 5 ft.

shift_table.name: Fortress Stance
shift_table.effect: +2 AC; allies within 5 ft gain half cover.

shift_table.name: Boulder Step
shift_table.effect: Creatures you move past make Dex save or fall prone; move through enemy spaces without OA.

techniques.name: Stone Fist
techniques.description: When you make an unarmed strike or a melee weapon attack, you can channel stone into the blow. The attack deals an extra 1d4 bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have its speed reduced by 5 feet until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Earthen Brace
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you plant your feet and draw stability from the stone beneath you. Until the start of your next turn, you cannot be knocked prone or forcibly moved, and you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

techniques.name: Pebble Burst
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you hurl a spray of sharp stones in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 piercing damage.

techniques.name: Stone Skin
techniques.description: As a reaction to taking damage, you harden your skin into a brief stone carapace. Reduce the triggering damage by 1d6 + Constitution modifier.

techniques.name: Tremor
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you strike the ground with enough force to shake it in a 10foot radius. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or lose 5 feet of movement until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Stoneguard
techniques.description: As a reaction to being hit by an attack, you can reduce the damage by 1d8 + your Constitution modifier.
techniques.usage: Uses equal to proficiency bonus / Long Rest

techniques.name: Boulder Toss
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you tear a chunk of rock from the earth or a nearby surface and hurl it at a creature within 60 feet. Make a ranged spell attack (Wisdom modifier + proficiency bonus). On a hit, the target takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

techniques.name: Stonegrasp
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you cause the ground beneath a creature within 30 feet to clutch it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Iron Step
techniques.description: Your movement is halved until the end of your turn, but you gain +2 AC and resistance to bludgeoning damage until the start of your next turn, as stone rises to meet each blow.

techniques.name: Crushing Weight
techniques.description: When you grapple a creature, you pin it with the force of the earth. The grappled creature takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be unable to stand up from prone while grappled.

techniques.name: Pulverizing Strike
techniques.description: When you land a melee weapon attack, you can channel stone energy to deal an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

techniques.name: Erupting Ground
techniques.description: As an action, you cause the ground to erupt beneath a creature within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d6 bludgeoning damage and be launched 10 feet into the air, landing prone. On a success, it takes half damage and is not moved.

techniques.name: Stone Shield Wall
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you raise a curved wall of stone 5 feet thick, 10 feet wide, and 10 feet tall within 15 feet. It provides three-quarters cover and is a barrier (AC 17, 50 HP). The wall lasts until destroyed or until you dismiss it as a bonus action.

techniques.name: Seismic Stomp
techniques.description: As an action, you stomp the ground with tremendous force. All creatures within 15 feet must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d8 bludgeoning damage and fall prone.

techniques.name: Petrifying Touch
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can attempt to turn it to stone. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its speed reduced by 20 feet and lose its reaction until the end of its next turn as stone begins to creep across its body.

techniques.name: Rock Wall
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you summon a wall of earth 5 feet high and 10 feet long within 30 feet of you. The wall provides half cover to creatures behind it and lasts until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Granite Shell
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you encase yourself in a shell of stone for 1 minute. You gain +3 AC and resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. While encased, your speed is reduced by 10 feet.

techniques.name: Landslide
techniques.description: As an action, you unleash a torrent of stone in a 30-foot line, 10 feet wide. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d8 bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage.

techniques.name: Stalactite Rain
techniques.description: As an action, you launch a barrage of stone spikes into the air that rain down on a 20-foot-radius area within 60 feet. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d8 piercing damage and be knocked prone. On a success, half damage only.

techniques.name: Stone Step
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you sink into the earth. Until the start of your next turn, you are invisible if you end your turn underground or behind a stone surface. You may reemerge from any stone surface or floor within 60 feet of where you submerged.

techniques.name: Tectonic Shift
techniques.description: As an action, every creature within 20 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone. Structures or objects within range take 2d8 bludgeoning damage.

techniques.name: Pillar of Stone
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you cause a 5-foot-diameter pillar of stone to erupt beneath a creature within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be launched 20 feet into the air, taking 4d6 bludgeoning damage when it lands and falling prone. On a success, it is pushed 5 feet to the side.

techniques.name: Living Fortress
techniques.description: As an action, you surround yourself in a 10-foot-radius dome of stone for 1 minute. The dome provides three-quarters cover to all creatures inside (including you). Creatures outside cannot pass through the walls without destroying them (AC 18, 100 HP). The dome can be dismissed as a bonus action.

techniques.name: Gravestone Mark
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you mark a creature within 60 feet. Stone rises around its feet, reducing its speed to 0 and causing it to be restrained until the end of your next turn. The creature may attempt a Strength saving throw to end the effect early as an action.

techniques.name: Stone Golem Shell
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you call chunks of stone and earth to orbit you for 1 minute. Whenever you are hit by an attack, one stone shard deflects to the attacker, dealing 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The shell has 3 shards before the effect ends.

techniques.name: Pillar Strike
techniques.description: When you hit with a melee weapon, you can create a 10-foot stone pillar beneath your target. The target is launched upward, taking 4d6 bludgeoning damage and falling prone.

techniques.name: Tectonic Rupture
techniques.description: As an action, you tear open a fissure 60 feet long and 5 feet wide within 30 feet of you. Creatures in the fissure's path must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall in, taking 6d10 bludgeoning damage from the fall and being restrained (unable to climb out without a successful DC 18 Athletics check as an action).

techniques.name: Mountain's Fury
techniques.description: For 1 minute, every melee attack you land deals an extra 2d10 bludgeoning damage, and each strike sends a shockwave to all creatures within 5 feet of the target, dealing 1d10 bludgeoning damage (Dexterity saving throw negates).

techniques.name: Stone Prison
techniques.description: As an action, you encase a creature within 30 feet in a cocoon of stone. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be encased — restrained and incapacitated for 1 minute. The stone prison has AC 18 and 60 HP; destroying it frees the creature. Huge or larger creatures are immune.

techniques.name: Earthquake Aura
techniques.description: As a bonus action, for 1 minute you constantly shake the earth in a 20-foot radius around you. All creatures in the area treat it as difficult terrain, and any creature that attempts to cast a spell must succeed on a Concentration check (DC 15) or lose the spell.

techniques.name: Cataclysm
techniques.description: As an action, you trigger a massive seismic event in a 120-foot radius centered on you. All creatures in the area take 8d10 bludgeoning damage (Dexterity saving throw halves). On a failed save, they are also knocked prone and stunned until the start of your next turn. Structures in the area take 4d10 bludgeoning damage.

techniques.name: Bedrock Form
techniques.description: For 1 hour, your body becomes living stone. You gain immunity to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks; a +5 bonus to AC; and immunity to being knocked prone or forcibly moved. Your speed is reduced by 10 feet. Melee attacks against you cause the attacker to take 1d10 bludgeoning damage from the impact.

techniques.name: Petrifying Gaze
techniques.description: As an action, you focus your elemental will on a creature within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be petrified for 1 hour. While petrified, it cannot take actions, bonus actions, reactions, or movement. The effect ends if the creature is attacked or takes damage. Huge or larger creatures have advantage on this saving throw.

techniques.name: Living Mountain
techniques.description: As an action, for 1 minute you grow to Large size (or one size larger if already Large or bigger). Your reach increases by 5 feet, your melee attacks deal an extra 3d10 bludgeoning damage, and whenever you move, each creature within 10 feet must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.

techniques.name: Continental Divide
techniques.description: As an action, you cause the earth to split apart in a 100-foot line, 10 feet wide, within 120 feet of you. Every creature in the line must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall into the fissure (taking 10d10 bludgeoning damage) and be trapped at the bottom until freed. On a success, they leap to one side and take no damage. The fissure persists for 1 hour.

features.name: Stoneform
features.description: When you activate your Elemental Shift, your skin hardens and the earth rises to meet your weight. While your Shift is active: your AC increases by 2 as stone plating forms over your skin; you cannot be knocked prone or forcibly moved against your will; you have resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

subclasses.name: Greenheart (Nature Attunement)
subclasses.description: Channel the living force of nature—poisoning, healing, entangling, and growing to overwhelm your enemies with life itself.

shift_table.name: Thornskin
shift_table.effect: Thorned vines on skin; creatures hitting you with melee take 1d6 piercing.

shift_table.name: Verdant Cloak
shift_table.effect: Advantage on Stealth; partial cover in foliage-heavy areas.

shift_table.name: Rooted Resilience
shift_table.effect: Bonus action: root yourself; cannot be knocked prone or moved.

shift_table.name: Bloom of Renewal
shift_table.effect: When healed, regain extra HP = proficiency bonus; allies within 10 ft gain half this bonus.

shift_table.name: Venomous Bloom
shift_table.effect: Action: poison spore cloud; Constitution save or poisoned for 1 minute.

shift_table.name: Symbiotic Regrowth
shift_table.effect: When an ally within 10 ft is healed, you recover Constitution mod HP.

techniques.name: Thorn Whip
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you lash out with a vine covered in thorns at a creature within 20 feet. Make a ranged spell attack (Wisdom modifier + proficiency bonus). On a hit, the target takes 1d6 piercing damage and is pulled 5 feet toward you.

techniques.name: Nature's Embrace
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you call a cluster of vines to wrap around you. Until the start of your next turn, if a creature attempts to grapple or move you, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or fail.

techniques.name: Spore Cloud
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you release a burst of disorienting spores in a 10-foot radius centered on you. Creatures in the area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on their next attack roll.

techniques.name: Barkskin
techniques.description: As a reaction to being hit by an attack, you harden your skin with wood-like bark. Reduce the triggering damage by 1d6 + Constitution modifier.

techniques.name: Wild Growth
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you cause plants to rapidly grow in a 10-foot-radius area within 30 feet. The area becomes difficult terrain until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Bramble Snare
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, vines lash from the earth, restraining the target. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained until the start of your next turn.
techniques.usage: Uses equal to proficiency bonus / Long Rest

techniques.name: Verdant Surge
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you channel healing energy from the living world. You or an ally within 20 feet regains 1d8 + Wisdom modifier hit points.

techniques.name: Poison Sap
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can infuse the wound with toxic plant sap. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of your next turn.

techniques.name: Root Trap
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you cause roots to erupt in a 10-foot-radius area within 30 feet. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Beast Sense
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you tap into the awareness of nearby animals. Until the start of your next turn, you cannot be surprised, and you have advantage on Perception checks.

techniques.name: Pollinating Strike
techniques.description: When you land a melee hit, you cause a burst of pollen to coat the target. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be blinded until the end of its next turn.

techniques.name: Thorned Armor
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you grow a coat of sharp thorns for 1 minute. Any creature that hits you with a melee attack takes 1d8 piercing damage. The thorns disappear if you are incapacitated.

techniques.name: Creeping Vine
techniques.description: As an action, you direct a fast-growing vine at a creature within 60 feet. The vine grabs the creature, and it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained and unable to move more than 10 feet from its current position for 1 minute (repeated Strength saves at the end of each of its turns).

techniques.name: Fungal Infection
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can infuse it with a magical fungus. The target takes 1d6 poison damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, it may make a Constitution saving throw to end the effect.

techniques.name: Natural Armor
techniques.description: As a bonus action, bark and moss form armor over your skin for 1 minute. Your AC increases by 2, and you have resistance to bludgeoning damage.

techniques.name: Nature's Sanctuary
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you create a 15-foot radius of flourishing plants around you. Allies inside the radius have advantage on death saving throws and recover 1d4 hit points at the start of their turn.

techniques.name: Grasping Forest
techniques.description: As an action, writhing plants erupt in a 20-foot radius centered on a point within 60 feet. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained for 1 minute (repeated saves at the end of each of their turns). Difficult terrain fills the entire area for the duration.

techniques.name: Vine Bridge
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you instantly grow a vine walkway up to 60 feet long and 5 feet wide through any terrain, including over gaps, pits, or across walls. The vine bridge lasts for 10 minutes and can support up to 500 pounds.

techniques.name: Wild Call
techniques.description: As an action, you call on nearby wildlife. 1d4 beasts of CR 1 or lower that are appropriate to the environment answer your call and act as allies for 1 minute. They use their own turns and obey your verbal commands.

techniques.name: Verdant Explosion
techniques.description: As an action, you cause a massive burst of plant growth to erupt from a point within 60 feet. Each creature in a 20-foot radius must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d8 piercing damage from thorns and be restrained for 1 round. On a success, half damage only.

techniques.name: Regenerative Sap
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you coat an ally within 30 feet in a restorative golden sap. For the next minute, that ally regains 1d10 hit points at the start of each of their turns.

techniques.name: Overgrowth
techniques.description: As an action, you cause massive plants to spring up across a 60-foot radius centered on you. The area is heavily overgrown: all movement through it costs triple, and creatures of your choice are unaffected. The overgrowth lasts for 10 minutes.

techniques.name: Plague of Thorns
techniques.description: As an action, you fling a barrage of razor thorns in a 30-foot cone. All creatures in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d8 piercing damage and be poisoned for 1 minute. On a success, half damage only.

techniques.name: Nature's Wrath
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you attune to the savage hunger of nature. For 1 minute, whenever you hit a creature with a weapon attack, writhing vines attempt to grab it — the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained until the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Tree Stride
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you meld into a tree or large plant within 5 feet and teleport to any other tree or large plant within 500 feet, emerging from it as a free action. You may do this up to three times before the effect ends.

techniques.name: Ironwood Form
techniques.description: As an action, for 1 minute your body is reinforced with ancient ironwood. You gain immunity to poison damage and the poisoned condition, resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks, and your unarmed strikes deal 2d8 bludgeoning damage.

techniques.name: Strangling Grove
techniques.description: As an action, a massive growth of strangling vines erupts in a 30-foot radius within 60 feet. Each creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained and take 3d10 bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute (repeated saves to escape).

techniques.name: Verdant Avatar
techniques.description: As an action, you take on the form of a nature spirit for 1 minute. You grow 1 size larger, your weapon attacks deal an extra 2d10 poison damage, and whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack, thorns rip at it for 2d6 piercing damage.

techniques.name: Primal Surge
techniques.description: As an action, you release the raw force of nature at a single creature within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 6d10 poison damage and be incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as plant life tears through its body. On a success, it takes half damage only.

techniques.name: Mass Healing Bloom
techniques.description: As an action, you cause blossoms of healing energy to bloom in a 30-foot radius centered on you. All allies in the area regain 4d8 + Wisdom modifier hit points and are cured of the poisoned condition.

techniques.name: World Tree
techniques.description: As an action, you summon a colossal ancient tree that takes root in a 60-foot radius centered on you. For 1 hour: the entire area is heavily overgrown difficult terrain for enemies; allies within the area regain 1d10 hit points at the start of their turns; and you can see through any plant within the area as if it were your own body.

techniques.name: Nature's Judgment
techniques.description: As an action, you direct the wrath of the living world at all enemies within 120 feet. Each enemy takes 8d10 poison damage and 4d10 piercing damage (Constitution saving throw halves). On a failed save, they are also restrained by vines for 1 minute.

techniques.name: Apex Predator
techniques.description: For 1 minute, you become the apex of natural force. You gain immunity to poison and disease; your attacks deal an extra 3d10 poison damage; you can take the Attack action twice as a bonus action once per turn; and all beasts within 120 feet are charmed by you for the duration.

techniques.name: Living Plague
techniques.description: As an action, you release a storm of magical spores in a 60-foot radius. All creatures of your choice in the area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned and incapacitated for 1 minute, taking 4d10 poison damage at the start of each of their turns (repeated saves to end the effect).

techniques.name: Rebirth
techniques.description: When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to release a burst of healing nature energy. You are restored to half your hit point maximum. All allies within 30 feet regain 3d10 hit points. Thorns erupt in a 15foot radius around you, dealing 4d10 piercing damage to all enemies (Dexterity saving throw halves).

features.name: Verdant Form
features.description: When you activate your Elemental Shift, your body becomes suffused with the restless energy of living things. While your Shift is active: you have resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against the poisoned condition; your movement ignores difficult terrain created by plants, roots, mud, or natural growth; tiny plants sprout in your wake — creatures that end their turn in a space you moved through this turn must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier) or have their speed reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

subclasses.name: Chronomancer (Time Attunement)
subclasses.description: Manipulate the flow of time itself—reversing moments, freezing enemies in temporal stasis, and giving your allies actions they never had.

shift_table.name: Temporal Blur
shift_table.effect: +2 AC (form flickers between moments).

shift_table.name: Speed Surge
shift_table.effect: Bonus action: Dash or Disengage; movement does not provoke OA this turn.

shift_table.name: Frozen Time
shift_table.effect: Freeze a small object in time for 1 minute (immune to damage/decay).

shift_table.name: Rewind Strike
shift_table.effect: On a miss with attack, reroll it; must use second roll.

shift_table.name: Echo of Action
shift_table.effect: Reaction: duplicate ally's movement from their last turn.

shift_table.name: Lingering Moment
shift_table.effect: At start of your turn, regain a spell slot of 3rd level or lower expended last round.

techniques.name: Slow Step
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you slow time around a creature within 30 feet. Until the start of your next turn, that creature's speed is halved and it cannot take reactions.

techniques.name: Quick Step
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you accelerate your own movements through time. You may immediately move up to half your speed. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

techniques.name: Temporal Blind
techniques.description: As a reaction when you are targeted by an attack, you briefly phase out of the attacker's timeline. The attack is made with disadvantage.

techniques.name: Echo Strike
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can cause a temporal echo of the strike to hit immediately after. The echo deals 1d6 force damage (no attack roll required).

techniques.name: Moment's Pause
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you briefly freeze a creature within 30 feet in time. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or lose its movement this turn only (it can still act otherwise).

techniques.name: Time Spike
techniques.description: As a reaction to an enemy entering your melee range, you can trap them in a temporal distortion. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be restrained for 1 round as time locks their movements.

techniques.name: Accelerate
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you or an ally within 30 feet gains an additional bonus action on their next turn. Their speed also increases by 20 feet until the end of that turn.

techniques.name: Temporal Disruption
techniques.description: As an action, you create a 15-foot-radius field of distorted time centered on a point within 60 feet until the start of your next turn. Creatures entering the field must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be slowed (as the slow spell) while in the field.

techniques.name: Rewind Step
techniques.description: As a reaction to being hit by an attack, you rewind yourself 1 second back in time, moving up to 10 feet to any unoccupied space you occupied since the start of your last turn. The attack misses if you are no longer in its range.

techniques.name: Future Sight
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you briefly scan the immediate future. Until the start of your next turn, you cannot be surprised, and you have advantage on the next attack roll or saving throw you make.

techniques.name: Temporal Reversal
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you rewind the last 6 seconds. Choose one creature within 30 feet to restore hit points equal to half the damage they took since the end of your last turn.

techniques.name: Time Stop Burst
techniques.description: As an action, time stops for all creatures except you for 1 round. For that round, you can move and take actions normally. At the start of your next turn, time resumes. Any effect you set in motion (objects thrown, etc.) takes effect when time resumes.

techniques.name: Aging Touch
techniques.description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can cause rapid aging. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its Strength and Dexterity scores reduced by 1d4 each until it finishes a long rest.

techniques.name: Temporal Copy
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you create a temporal copy of yourself from 6 seconds ago. The copy appears in the space you occupied 6 seconds (1 round) ago and acts as an ally. It has your stats but 1 HP, cannot use techniques, and vanishes at the start of your next turn.

techniques.name: Phase Lag
techniques.description: As an action, you impose a 6-second lag on a creature within 60 feet. For 1 minute, all of that creature's actions take effect 1 round after it attempts them (it declares an action, but the effect happens at the end of its next turn). Wisdom saving throw negates.

techniques.name: Time Sink
techniques.description: You create a 10-foot radius time dilation field for 1 minute. Enemies inside the field have their speed halved, and attacks they make are rolled at disadvantage.

techniques.name: Temporal Storm
techniques.description: As an action, you create a storm of fractured time in a 20-foot radius centered on a point within 60 feet for 1 minute. Creatures in the storm are affected unpredictably: roll a d4 for each at the start of their turns. On a 1, they are stunned; on a 2-3, they are slowed; on a 4, they are hasted (as the haste spell).

techniques.name: Aged Armor
techniques.description: As a reaction to being hit, you rapidly age the attack in time, turning the weapon or projectile brittle. Reduce the damage by 2d10 + Wisdom modifier as the force of the impact dissipates in shifted time.

techniques.name: Déjà Vu
techniques.description: As a bonus action, you force a creature within 60 feet to relive its last turn exactly. It must take the same movement, action, and bonus action it took on its previous turn (Wisdom saving throw negates). Effectively, the creature has no choice in its turn this round.

techniques.name: Rewind Zone
techniques.description: As an action, you rewind the state of the battlefield in a 20-foot radius: all creatures in the area are teleported to wherever they stood at the start of the previous round, and all conditions affecting them from this round are removed. Wisdom saving throw allows a creature to resist being moved.

techniques.name: Parallel Future
techniques.description: You glimpse a possible outcome of an action. After rolling a d20 for an attack or ability check, you can roll again and choose either result.

techniques.name: Temporal Immunity
